prerequisites: A Box account with access to the Box Developer Console (a free developer account grants Developer Console access).
A Box application created in the Developer Console to obtain its Client ID and Client Secret.
The Client ID of the Box application (configured as the client_id field).
The Client Secret of the Box application (configured as the client_secret field).
The Box user to operate as (configured as the user field).

authMethods.name: Box app credentials (Client ID and Client Secret)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate using a Box application's Client ID and Client Secret, which are issued when you create an app in the Box Developer Console. These map to the connector's client_id and client_secret secret fields. Box uses an Access Token derived from these app credentials to identify and authorize API calls.

setupSteps.title: Create a Box application in the Developer Console
setupSteps.body: Log into Box and open the Developer Console. Select Platform Apps, then click New App and choose an authentication type for the app. Box notes that once you make a selection you cannot change to a different authentication method without creating a new application.

setupSteps.title: Retrieve the Client ID and Client Secret
setupSteps.body: Open your app's Configuration page in the Developer Console to obtain the Client ID and Client Secret. Box authentication uses these app credentials, exchanged for an Access Token that identifies the user on each API call.

setupSteps.title: Provide credentials and the target user to the connector
setupSteps.body: Set client_id to your Box application's Client ID and client_secret to its Client Secret. Set the user field to the Box user the connector should operate as. All three fields are required.
